captured_log.download_method: gh api repos/konard/private-logs/git/blobs/<sha> --jq .content | base64 -d (token expired on the original raw URL; fetched via authenticated Blobs API)

online_sources.title: Child process | Node.js Documentation (detached option, process group leader, unref)
online_sources.used_for: Confirms `detached: true` makes the child a new process-group leader, isolating it from the parent's controlling-terminal SIGINT; and that the parent keeps waiting unless subprocess.unref() is called.

online_sources.title: Process | Node.js Documentation (signal events, multiple listeners)
online_sources.used_for: Confirms Node runs ALL registered listeners for a signal, and that installing a SIGINT listener removes Node's default exit behavior.

online_sources.title: How to handle process signals in Node.js (CoreUI)
online_sources.used_for: Background on registering and coordinating multiple signal handlers.

online_sources.title: How to Build a Graceful Shutdown Handler in Node.js (OneUptime)
online_sources.used_for: Best practice: a single shutdown manager other modules hook into; idempotent isShuttingDown guard; reserve process.exit() for forced/timeout paths.

online_sources.title: node-graceful (npm)
online_sources.used_for: Existing library that lets listeners return promises to delay exit until cleanup completes — same contract we implement in-house.

online_sources.title: kill-with-style (npm)
online_sources.used_for: Existing library for reliably killing a process tree/group — relevant to the force-kill (second-interrupt) path.

online_sources.title: SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers prevent graceful shutdown (openai/openai-agents-js#184)
online_sources.used_for: Real-world example of a dependency's own signal handler racing the application's graceful shutdown — the same class of bug as the hive/exit-handler double-handler race.
